comments.body: Implemented (commit 39da418cc). Wrote docs/adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md (reuse boundary, region gate tri-state fail-safe, surfaced-only, permanent-within-key drop + priority escape, typed keyspaces, content-addressed resilience, no-dso-arbiter decision w/ inline term defs, implementation-status map); added Code-review novelty convergence section to docs/review-kernel.md; cross-linked docs/reuse-surface.md. Awaiting Gerrit Verified before close.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Pushed to Gerrit as change 478 (stacked top). Awaiting Verified + LLM-Review.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
rebar records cross-cutting review-gate design in ADRs (plan-review's novelty rising floor is
ADR 0008). The code-review novelty convergence mechanism — its divergence from plan-review
(per-citation region gate; typed local/Gerrit keyspaces; content-addressed resilient region
detection), the reuse boundary (shared kernel primitives vs gate-specific orchestration), and the
deliberate NON-additions — needs a durable ADR + docs so future work does not re-litigate or
accidentally fork the novelty definition.

## What
Write **`docs/adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md`** (status Accepted — it documents
DECISIONS that are IMPLEMENTED on this epic's stack) and cross-link the docs. The ADR documents,
each citing its backing implementation module so a reader can verify it:

- **Reuse the novelty sub-call + `rising_floor_drop`** — the shared kernel primitives
  (`review_kernel.verify.novelty_model` / `NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS` / `reshape_novelties`,
  `review_kernel.decide.novelty` / `rising_floor_drop`) reused UNCHANGED. Code-review-specific
  orchestration: `src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py::score_code_novelty` +
  `apply_region_gated_floor`, the `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code_review_novelty.md` prompt + the
  `code-novelty` reviewer id, and the `code_review_novelty` contract registered in
  `src/rebar/llm/code_review/contracts.py`.
- **The new `region_gated_floor` conjunct + its tri-state fail-safe (UNKNOWN == raise)** —
  `src/rebar/llm/code_review/region_gate.py` (constants `REGION_UNCHANGED` / `REGION_CHANGED` /
  `REGION_UNKNOWN` + the detector); the floor drops iff `rising_floor_drop(...)` AND region is
  `REGION_UNCHANGED`, and CHANGED/UNKNOWN always raise (fail-safe direction).
- **Surfaced-only prior set** — the reader `latest_code_review_result`
  (`src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py`) returns only the persisted `blocking` + `advisory`
  buckets, so a dropped finding never re-enters the novelty prior set (the code-review counterpart
  of child c3e4 / bug old-frilly-plankton).
- **Permanent low-impact drop = the intended compromise; the priority floor is the escape hatch.**
  Define "permanent" PRECISELY: the drop persists across RE-REVIEWS FOR THE SAME KEY (session or
  change) — it does NOT reset per review the way plan-review's floor re-scores each run — because
  the surfaced-only reader excludes a dropped finding from the prior set, so on recurrence it scores
  as NOVEL and is dropped again (as long as it stays low-priority + region-unchanged). Permanence is
  WITHIN one keyspace, not cross-key. The priority floor (`verify.novelty_priority_floor`) is the
  escape hatch: a higher-impact finding is never eligible, so it keeps surfacing. Trust the priority
  score — no categorical criterion exemption.
- **Typed session/change keyspaces, and why local must NOT seed a change's first Gerrit review** —
  `session:<id>` (local, `code-review: session:{id}`) vs `change:<id>` (Gerrit,
  `code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}`); the reader (`latest_code_review_result`) matches only
  its own title scheme, so the keyspaces are disjoint and a prior LOCAL review can never seed a
  change's FIRST Gerrit review.
- **Content-addressed region detection and its resilience to rebase/force-push** — the artifact
  stores `{path: sha256}` (reusing `plan_review.attest._hash_file`, computed in
  `code_review/sidecar.py::reviewed_file_hashes`); comparison keys on CONTENT, not a reachable
  commit, so it survives a rebase/force-push. File-level in v1; line-level deferred.
- **Explicit NON-additions (AC2).** Record the decision to add NO "dso-style
  arbiter/relation-taxonomy/oscillation-loop", DEFINING those terms inline so the rejection is
  evaluable: a **DSO-style arbiter** = a standalone decision/signal orchestrator that ADJUDICATES
  between competing findings/signals; a **relation-taxonomy** = a typed model of relationships
  BETWEEN findings (duplicates/supersedes/refines/…); an **oscillation-loop** = a detector that
  tracks a finding surfacing→dropping→resurfacing across runs to break cycles. The ADR records WHY
  the lightweight novelty floor suffices instead: a permanent, priority-gated drop of novel
  low-impact findings on unchanged regions monotonically narrows the surfaced set across
  patchsets without modeling oscillation or inter-finding relationships — so the heavier machinery
  buys no demonstrated benefit for added failure modes. This positively discharges the epic's
  original oscillation/convergence-signal criterion as a DESIGN decision, not a deferral.

Also update the docs to cross-link: **`docs/review-kernel.md`** (its "Code-review consumer"
section — documenting the novelty convergence behavior: carryover labeling, region-gated drop,
keying, resilience) and **`docs/reuse-surface.md`** (pointing the shared kernel primitives at the
code-review-specific region-gate seam).

## Scope
- `docs/adr/0037-code-review-novelty-convergence.md` (NEW), `docs/review-kernel.md` (the
  "Code-review consumer" section — the code-review gate doc), and `docs/reuse-surface.md`.
- Documents the IMPLEMENTED behavior landed by c639 / 3bff / e51b + the c3e4 surfaced-only fix.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ADR 0037 captures the design decisions above, INCLUDING the deliberate divergence from
      plan-review's whole-artifact floor (the per-citation region gate), each element citing its
      backing implementation module.
- [ ] ADR 0037 records the explicit decision to add NO dso-style arbiter / relation-taxonomy /
      oscillation-loop (terms DEFINED inline) and why the lightweight novelty floor suffices.
- [ ] ADR 0037 defines "permanent" drop precisely (within-key, via the surfaced-only prior set) and
      how it diverges from plan-review's per-review re-scoring.
- [ ] `docs/review-kernel.md` (Code-review consumer section) documents the novelty convergence
      behavior (carryover labeling, region-gated drop, keying, resilience).
- [ ] The docs cross-link the shared kernel primitives vs the code-review-specific region gate
      (`docs/reuse-surface.md`).
compiled_state.title: ADR + docs: code-review novelty convergence (mirror plan-review ADR 0008)
